basic_details.job_title: IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026 Notification Out – Walk in for 92 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation (IRCTC), North Zone
basic_details.short_information: The latest Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation (IRCTC) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Hospitality Monitor posts! Published on 13 May 2026 at irctc.com, this new recruitment drive offers 92 vacancies for eligible candidates. Applicants within the age range of Max: 27 years and possessing a B.Sc. in Hospitality and Hotel Administration or BBA/MBA in Culinary Arts, or equivalent qualifications, can apply through a Walk-in Interview from 03 June 2026 to 23 June 2026. Find all details about the IRCTC Hospitality Monitor new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, walk-in interview

important_dates.interview_date: 03, 04 & 05 June 2026 (Wednesday, Thursday & Friday) - 11:00 AM to 05:00 PM

age_limit.age_details: Contractual - 2 years initially, extendable for 1 year based on requirement and satisfactory performance
age_limit.details: The upper age limit is 27 years for Unreserved (UR) candidates as on 01-05-2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for reserved category candidates. Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply.

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: B.Sc. in Hospitality and Hotel Administration
B.Sc. Hotel Management and Catering Science

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 01-05-2026. Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. Freshers with the relevant educational qualification are eligible to apply; prior experience is not mandatory. Candidates should ensure that they fulfill the eligibility criteria prescribed for the post before applying. | Educational Qualification

vacancy_details.Reservation: SC-15%, ST-7.5%, OBC-NCL-7%, EWS-10%, PwBD-4%, Ex-Servicemen-10% (as per Govt. of India policy)

salary_details.total ctc: Rs. 30,000/- per month (inclusive of Statutory Deductions)
salary_details.daily allowance (on-duty on-board in tra: Rs. 350/- per day (100% for more than 12 hours; 70% for 6 to 12 hours; 30% for less than 6 hours)
salary_details.lodging charges: Rs. 240/- only if night stay is involved at outstation
salary_details.medical insurance: On per month basis as per extant policy (Reimbursable on submission of valid documents)
salary_details.security deposit: Rs. 25,000/- (Demand Draft payable to IRCTC Ltd, New Delhi) to be furnished by selected candidates
salary_details.details: The selected candidates will be paid a total CTC of Rs. 30,000/- per month (inclusive of statutory deductions) along with other applicable allowances as detailed below. No TA/DA will be paid to candidates for attending the walk-in interview. Selected candidates have to furnish a security deposit of Rs. 25,000/- in the form of a Demand Draft payable to IRCTC Ltd, New Delhi.

selection_process: New Delhi IRCTC, North Zone: B-148, 11th Floor, Statesman House, Barakhamba Road, Connaught Place, New Delhi - 110001 03, 04 & 05 June 2026 (Wednesday, Thursday & Friday) 11:00 AM to 05:00 PM
Srinagar, J&K Institute of Hotel Management, Rajbagh, Srinagar, Jammu & Kashmir - 190008 11 & 12 June 2026 (Thursday & Friday) 11:00 AM to 05:00 PM
Chandigarh IRCTC, Regional Office: SCO - 150, 151, 152, Ground Floor, Sector 34-A, Chandigarh - 160022 15 & 16 June 2026 (Monday & Tuesday) 11:00 AM to 05:00 PM

Jaipur, Rajasthan IRCTC, Regional Office: 1st Floor (Above Union Bank), Jeevan Prakash Building, LIC, Ambedkar Circle, Bhawani Singh Road, Jaipur - 302005 18 & 19 June 2026 (Thursday & Friday) 11:00 AM to 05:00 PM
Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh IRCTC, Regional Office: Paryatan Bhawan, 2nd Floor, C-13, Vipin Khand, Gomti Nagar, Lucknow - 226010 23 & 24 June 2026 (Tuesday & Wednesday) 11:00 AM to 05:00 PM

The selection process for IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026 is through Walk-in Interview. Candidates are required to fill in the application form (attached with the notification) and submit it at the venue of the interview along with original documents
one set of attested photocopies of required documents
and two recent passport size photographs. Based on credentials and performance in the personal interview
candidates will be shortlisted. The offer of engagement shall be issued to suitable candidates in order of merit and based on number of vacancies

Engagement is subject to the candidate being medically fit as prescribed by IRCTC. The contract may be terminated by giving one month’s notice by either side. If performance during the contract period is not found satisfactory
the contract may be terminated without any notice.
Note: A candidate can appear for interview at any one convenient place only. In case of extension of interview date
candidates will have to arrange their own stay and food. Any corrigendum/clarifications to this notification (including date & place of interview)
will be uploaded on the IRCTC website (www.irctc.com) only and no separate press coverage will be given in newspapers.
how_to_apply: Candidates are required to fill in the application form (Proforma attached with the official notification) duly complete in all respects and submit it at the venue of the walk-in interview. No online application process is involved - candidates must appear in person at any one of the listed venues on the specified dates. The completed application form has to be submitted at the venue for verification along with original documents, one set of attested copies of requisite documents, and two recent passport size photographs.

Those working in Government/PSUs may apply through proper channel or submit NOC at the time of interview or should submit a proper relieving letter from their present employer in the event of selection in IRCTC. No TA/DA will be paid to candidates for attending the walk-in interview. Candidate can appear for interview at any one convenient place only.
Documents Required at Walk-in Interview (Originals in chronological order, along with one set of self-attested photocopies):

Graduation year-wise all Marksheets & Certificate/Degree (Provisional Certificate/Degree from concerned University may also be considered). Candidates passed out from IHMs should carry all six semesters marksheets, consolidated marksheet issued from IGNOU & degree/certificate issued from IGNOU.
Post-Graduation or any other higher academic/education (year-wise all Marksheets & Certificate), if applicable
All Experience Certificates in chronological order (if any)
Caste certificate supporting SC/ST/OBC category (OBC certificate should be for non-creamy layer and not more than 1 year old)
Proof of Identity & Proof of Address

In absence of any of the above documents in original, the candidate will not be allowed to appear in the walk-in interview. Candidates should carry one set of self-attested photocopy of all above documents along with all originals.
Walk-in Interview Venue (Delhi): IRCTC, North Zone: B-148, 11th Floor, Statesman House, Barakhamba Road, Connaught Place, New Delhi - 110001
important_instructions: Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. This engagement is purely contractual in nature and will not entitle any candidate to claim regular/permanent employment in IRCTC. Freshers with relevant educational qualification are eligible; prior experience is not mandatory. The cut-off date for age, qualification, experience and other purposes is 01-05-2026. Candidate can appear for interview at any one convenient place only. No TA/DA will be paid to candidates for attending the walk-in interview. Selected candidates have to furnish a security deposit of Rs. 25,000/- in the form of a Demand Draft payable to IRCTC Ltd, New Delhi. OBC certificate should be for non-creamy layer category and should not be more than 1 year old. If performance during the contract period is not found satisfactory, the contract may be terminated without any notice. In case it is found at any stage of selection or even after engagement that the candidate has furnished false or incorrect information or suppressed any material facts, their candidature/services will be summarily terminated. IRCTC reserves the right to cancel/amend the advertisement and/or the selection process at any time without prior notice. The number of vacancies (including Reserve Panel) may increase or decrease depending on actual requirement of IRCTC at the time of engagement. Engagement is subject to the candidate being medically fit as prescribed by IRCTC. Any corrigendum/clarifications to this notification will be uploaded on www.irctc.com only; no separate press coverage will be given in newspapers.

faq.question: Q1. What is IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026 is a contractual recruitment drive by Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation (IRCTC), North Zone, to fill 92 Hospitality Monitor posts for on‑board and static catering services for an initial period of 2 years, extendable by 1 year based on requirement and performance.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are announced under IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 92 vacancies have been notified for the post of Hospitality Monitor on a contractual basis under IRCTC North Zone, with reservation applicable for SC, ST, OBC‑NCL, EWS, PwBD and Ex‑servicemen as per Government of India rules.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility criteria for IRCTC Hospitality Monitor 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be Indian nationals, not more than 27 years of age as on 01‑05‑2026, and possess a full‑time B.Sc. in Hospitality and Hotel Administration, BBA/MBA in Culinary Arts, B.Sc. Hotel Management and Catering Science, or MBA (Tourism and Hotel Management) from recognized institutes/universities as specified in the notification, while freshers with these qualifications are also eligible.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit and age relaxation for IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 27 years for UR candidates as on 01‑05‑2026, with relaxations of 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (Non‑Creamy Layer), 10 years for PwBD, and additional relaxation for Ex‑servicemen equal to their defence service plus 3 years as per Government of India norms.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of a walk‑in Personal Interview where candidates are assessed based on their educational credentials, relevant skills and performance in the interview, and a merit list is prepared for engagement subject to document verification and medical fitness as per IRCTC standards.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary and allowances for IRCTC Hospitality Monitor (North Zone)?
faq.answer: Selected Hospitality Monitors will receive a consolidated remuneration of Rs. 30,000 per month (inclusive of statutory deductions), along with daily allowance for on‑board duty, eligible lodging charges for outstation night stay, national holiday allowance as per policy, medical insurance reimbursement, and they must also deposit a refundable security of Rs. 25,000 in the form of a demand draft in favour of IRCTC Ltd, New Delhi.

faq.question: Q7. What are the walk‑in interview dates and venues for IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Walk‑in interviews will be conducted from 03 June 2026 to 24 June 2026 between 11:00 AM and 05:00 PM at multiple venues including IRCTC North Zone office in New Delhi, Institute of Hotel Management in Srinagar, IRCTC regional offices at Chandigarh, Jaipur and Lucknow, and candidates can appear at any one convenient centre only.

faq.question: Q8. What is the place of posting for selected IRCTC Hospitality Monitors?
faq.answer: Selected candidates may be posted anywhere under IRCTC North Zone including Delhi‑NCR, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Uttarakhand, Chandigarh, Jammu & Kashmir, and can also be deployed at other locations across India at the discretion of IRCTC.

faq.question: Q9. What are the main job responsibilities of an IRCTC Hospitality Monitor?
faq.answer: Hospitality Monitors are responsible for supervising and monitoring food production, quality and service in trains and units, ensuring adherence to IRCTC’s hospitality standards and statutory norms, coordinating with staff and vendors, handling passenger complaints and feedback, maintaining reports, and supporting overall customer service and hygiene compliance.

faq.question: Q10. How can candidates apply for IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There is no online application; candidates must download and fill the prescribed application form attached with the notification, and submit it in person at the chosen walk‑in interview venue along with original documents, one set of self‑attested photocopies, and two recent passport photos on the scheduled date and time.
